prerequisites: An active Intruder account at app.intruder.io with access to the My Account settings.
An Intruder API access token, which is a personal access token linked to your Intruder user account.
Your Intruder account password, required to confirm token creation in the portal.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 Bearer Access Token
authMethods.summary: Maps to the connector's required access_token secret field. The Intruder API uses OAuth 2.0 bearer token authentication: the personal access token is sent in the Authorization header as 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' with every request. The connector stores this single token value in access_token.

setupSteps.title: Sign in and open account settings
setupSteps.body: Log in to the Intruder portal and navigate to My Account, where you will find the API Access Tokens section.

setupSteps.title: Create an API access token
setupSteps.body: In the API Access Tokens section, give the token a meaningful name that identifies the tool that will use it, and optionally set an expiry date. Leaving the expiry blank creates a token that does not expire. Enter your account password and confirm to generate the token.

setupSteps.title: Copy the token immediately
setupSteps.body: Copy the generated access token as soon as it is shown. The token is displayed only once and cannot be retrieved again afterward. If you lose it, generate a replacement.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied token into the connector's required access_token field. The connector sends it as a bearer token in the Authorization header on every API request.

sources.title: Intruder Developer Hub - Creating an access token
Intruder Help Center - Creating API access tokens
